title: GCHandle double-free race in WinHttpRequestState dispose
category: race condition -> GCHandle double-free across the managed ↔ native (WinHTTP) boundary
crash_site: intermittent crash / 'GCHandle was previously freed' / access violation during HttpClient request teardown over WinHTTP, surfacing in native WinHTTP callback handling, not at an obvious free site.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: the GCHandle field and its Free() call (looks like the bug) and the IDisposable finalizer. The defect is the non-atomic check-then-set of _disposed in Dispose, not the Free itself.
ground_truth.fix_summary: Dispose used `if (_disposed) return; _disposed = true;` with `_disposed` only volatile, not atomic. Two threads (request completion + cancellation) could both pass the check and both Free the GCHandle -> double free. Fix: make the disposed transition atomic (Interlocked) so exactly one caller frees the handle.

admission.note: Authored from PR #125293 body+diff. crash != cause: AV surfaces in native WinHTTP callback handling; cause is a non-atomic volatile check-then-set in WinHttpRequestState.Dispose letting two threads both Free the GCHandle. Decoy = the GCHandle.Free call. Single file -> watch contamination. C# managed handle lifetime <-> native WinHTTP. Run baseline first.

provenance: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/pull/125293 — Fix GCHandle double-free race in WinHttpRequestState; fix 28c5a4dcc838, base 133f6a80839a.
